code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test12-JUN-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further renal evaluation and correlation with clinical findings, including assessment for possible acute or chronic kidney disease and review of glycemic status.
